laws.actName: Government of National Capital Territory of Delhi Act, 1991
laws.title: Qualifications for membership of Legislative Assembly
laws.description: A person shall not be qualified to be chosen to fill a seat in the Legislative Assembly unless he is a citizen of India and makes and subscribes before some person authorised in that behalf by the Election Commission an oath or affirmation according to the form set out for the purpose in the Schedule, is not less than twenty-five years of age, and possesses such other qualifications as may be prescribed in that behalf by or under any law made by Parliament.
laws.simpleExplanation: Candidates must be at least twenty-five years of age, an Indian citizen, take an oath or affirmation, and meet other prescribed qualifications.

laws.actName: Government of National Capital Territory of Delhi Act, 1991
laws.title: Disqualifications for membership
laws.description: Specifies disqualifications for being chosen as, and for being, a member of the Legislative Assembly, such as holding any office of profit under the Government of India, a state, or a union territory, or if disqualified under article 102 of the Constitution.
laws.simpleExplanation: A person cannot be a member if they hold an office of profit or are disqualified under the Indian Constitution.

laws.actName: Government of National Capital Territory of Delhi Act, 1991
laws.title: Penalty for sitting and voting before making oath or affirmation or when not qualified or when disqualified
laws.description: If a person sits or votes as a member of the Legislative Assembly before he has complied with the requirements of section 12 or when he knows that he is not qualified or that he is disqualified for membership thereof, he shall be liable in respect of each day on which he so sits or votes to a penalty of five hundred rupees to be recovered as a debt due to the Union.
laws.simpleExplanation: A penalty of 500 rupees per day is charged for sitting or voting in the Assembly without taking the oath, or when unqualified or disqualified.

punishments.description: Penalty of five hundred rupees to be recovered as a debt due to the Union for each day on which he so sits or votes.

relatedSections.description: Requires oath or affirmation before taking seat
